reasoning: Exchange rates function largely as random walks, meaning the probability of an increase over any given period is generally close to 50%. The current value as of May 9 (1.3925) reflects a slight rebound after a month of the CAD strengthening against the USD (falling from highs above 1.45 in March). The FRED statistical tool estimates a 29% chance of being above this threshold by May 18, weighting the recent negative linear trend. However, due to the last two days of upward spikes and the nature of exchange rates resisting predictable short-term trends, I adjust the short-term probability higher to 0.42. As the forecast horizon increases (1 month, 3 months, 6 months), uncertainties around the differential trajectories of the Bank of Canada and Federal Reserve interest rates compound, bringing the probability closer to an uninformative 50% baseline.
